seo.title: Context Window Management for AI Agents in Claude Tool Use Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix context window management for ai agents in Claude Tool Use agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Claude Tool Use agents, Context Window Management issues arise when long tool call chains and iterative reasoning exceed the model's 200K token limit, causing truncation of critical history and state loss. This manifests as agents repeating failed tool calls, hallucinating prior results, or abruptly abandoning tasks mid-execution. The problem is exacerbated by Claude's verbose XML-formatted tool responses that rapidly consume context during agentic loops.

howItManifests.scenario: Multi-step research agent with 10+ tool calls
howItManifests.symptom: Agent repeats identical web search queries after 5th iteration
howItManifests.impact: Infinite loop, 30min+ execution time, incomplete final answer

howItManifests.scenario: Code debugging agent iterating on fixes
howItManifests.symptom: Forgets previous error messages, reintroduces same bugs
howItManifests.impact: Divergent code evolution, never converges to working solution

howItManifests.scenario: Parallel tool execution with result aggregation
howItManifests.symptom: Only processes first 2/5 tool results, ignores the rest

detection.name: Token Overflow Monitor
detection.description: Tracks cumulative token usage across message history and alerts when approaching 80% of context window

detection.name: Tool Call Repetition Detector
detection.description: Identifies when agent calls same tool with identical inputs consecutively

detection.name: History Truncation Check
detection.description: Verifies if critical prior tool results are referenced in current reasoning

fixes.name: Dynamic Summary Compression
fixes.description: Condenses tool results into structured summaries before adding to context

fixes.name: Stateful Memory Manager
fixes.description: Extracts and persists key state variables across context resets

fixes.name: Chunked Execution Pipeline
fixes.description: Breaks long tasks into explicit phases with context reset between
fixes.code:
def chunked_agent_execution(client, task, max_iterations_per_chunk=8):
    phases = ['research', 'analyze', 'synthesize']
    final_result = ''
    
    for phase in phases:
        messages = [{'role': 'user', 'content': f"Phase: {phase}. Task: {task}. Prior: {final_result}"}]
        
        for _ in range(max_iterations_per_chunk):
            response = client.messages.create(
                model="claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620",
                max_tokens=1024,
                messages=messages,
                tools=[your_tools]
            )
            messages.append({'role': 'assistant', 'content': response.content})
            
            if not response.content or response.stop_reason == 'end_turn':
                final_result += f"\n{phase}: " + ' '.join([b.text for b in response.content if b.type == 'text'])
                break
    
    return final_result

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Context Window Management for AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex automatically monitors Claude Tool Use agents for context overflow by tracking token consumption in real-time across tool call chains. It detects repetition patterns, missing tool result references, and truncation symptoms, then applies intelligent remediation like dynamic summarization and state extraction without manual intervention. The platform wraps Anthropic's messages.create() calls with vex.verify(), providing observability into context health and automatic recovery paths.

Vex's Claude-specific detectors parse XML tool_use blocks and tool_result responses to maintain precise token accounting, including the verbose formatting overhead unique to Anthropic's API. When issues are detected, Vex can trigger context compression, state migration to external memory, or task rechunking, ensuring agents complete reliably even in 50+ iteration chains. This eliminates the need for custom monitoring logic in production agent deployments.

Integration takes <5 minutes via pip install anthropic vex-sdk, with full observability dashboard showing context utilization trends, failure patterns, and ROI from automated fixes.
vexAutomation.code:
import anthropic
import vex

client = anthropic.Anthropic()
vex_client = vex.Vex(api_key="your-vex-key")

@vex_client.verify(test_name="claude-context-management")
def reliable_claude_agent(task: str):
    messages = [{'role': 'user', 'content': task}]
    
    while True:
        # Vex automatically monitors context window
        response = client.messages.create(
            model="claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620",
            max_tokens=1024,
            messages=messages,
            tools=[{"name": "search", "input_schema": {...}}]
        )
        
        messages.extend([{'role': 'assistant', 'content': response.content}])
        
        # Vex auto-applies fixes: compression, state extraction
        if response.stop_reason == 'tool_use':
            tool_calls = [b for b in response.content if b.type == 'tool_use']
            tool_results = execute_tools(tool_calls)
            messages.append({'role': 'user', 'content': tool_results})
        else:
            return response.content[0].text

# Run with automatic context management
result = reliable_claude_agent("Complex multi-step research task")
